india_regulations.name: India Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS)
india_regulations.authority: Bureau of Energy Efficiency / Ministry of Power

intensity_targets.Overall avg: ~3% intensity reduction in first compliance cycle

india_regulations.penalty: Purchase CCCs at 2× average market price for shortfall
india_regulations.voluntary_mechanism: Available from June 2025 for non-obligated entities
india_regulations.financial_impact: Companies missing targets face ₹1,200–1,800/tonne penalty (2× market rate)
india_regulations.brsr_link: CCTS data feeds directly into BRSR P6 GHG disclosures

india_regulations.name: SEBI BRSR Core Expansion

timeline.FY2024-25: Top 250 listed companies — mandatory + third-party assurance
timeline.FY2025-26: Top 500 listed entities — mandatory BRSR Core
timeline.FY2026-27: Top 1,000 listed entities — mandatory BRSR Core

india_regulations.value_chain: Top 250 must disclose upstream/downstream partners ≥2% of purchases/sales, capped at 75% of total
india_regulations.assurance: SEBI shifted from 'assurance' to 'assessment or assurance' (March 2025) — expands qualified professionals
india_regulations.new_requirement: Green credits disclosure added as leadership indicator under P6 for companies and top 10 value chain partners
india_regulations.financial_impact: Non-compliance risk: SEBI enforcement action, delisting risk for top-tier companies

india_regulations.name: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)

financial_impact.steel_export_drop: Indian steel/aluminium exports to EU dropped 24.4% YoY in FY2024-25
financial_impact.price_cut_needed: 15–22% price reduction needed to stay competitive
financial_impact.example: Mid-sized exporter shipping 50,000 tonnes faces €3–4 million annual CBAM exposure
financial_impact.steel_carbon_cost: €56–80 per tonne steel at €80/tonne EU ETS price (India avg 2.5t CO2/t vs EU 1.5-1.8t)

india_regulations.compliance_deadline: First CBAM declaration for 2026 imports due September 30, 2027
india_regulations.penalty: Default emission values 30–80% higher than actual if not declared
india_regulations.brsr_link: Companies must produce CBAM-compatible GHG data — direct overlap with BRSR P6

india_regulations.name: EU CSRD Omnibus Revision

revised_thresholds.third_country_parent: €450M EU turnover + EU subsidiary with €200M turnover

india_regulations.india_impact: Indian companies with EU operations or EU-listed subsidiaries are affected

india_regulations.name: RBI Climate Risk Disclosure Framework
india_regulations.status: Draft as of April 2026 (delayed from late 2025)
india_regulations.scope: Commercial banks, all-India financial institutions, top-layer NBFCs
india_regulations.financial_impact: Banks must assess climate risk in lending — affects credit availability for high-carbon sectors

india_regulations.name: India Green Credit Programme
india_regulations.brsr_link: Now mandatory leadership indicator in BRSR P6 for top 250 companies

IFRS_S2.name: IFRS S2 Climate-related Disclosures
IFRS_S2.alignment: Builds on and integrates TCFD recommendations + SASB industry standards
IFRS_S2.india_relevance: SEBI BRSR Core designed to converge with IFRS S2 over time

TCFD.name: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures
TCFD.india_relevance: Predecessor framework; SEBI BRSR Section B aligns with TCFD pillars

GHG_PROTOCOL.name: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard

scopes.Scope 3: All other indirect emissions across value chain (15 categories)

GHG_PROTOCOL.india_relevance: Referenced by SEBI BRSR P6 and CCTS baseline measurement

SBTi.name: Science-Based Targets initiative
SBTi.india_adoption: Growing among top BSE 500 companies; required for some EU supply chain partners
SBTi.financial_signal: SBTi commitment → lower cost of capital, better ESG ratings, EU market access
